title: hieracium bupleuroides subsp. dolense
intro: Hieracium bupleuroides subsp. dolense, also known as dolene hawkweed, is a rare subspecies of the Hieracium bupleuroides plant, belonging to the dandelion family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits associated with Hieracium bupleuroides subsp. dolense include its potential use in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
